commit.message:
docs: promote MEMORY.md lessons to CLAUDE.md + routine review in implement-issue

CLAUDE.md changes:
- Fix stale waitForTimeout value: 200ms → 1000ms (seed bulkAdd takes longer)
- Add: IndexedDB runtime DB name is 'SpacedLearning' (needed for manual deleteDatabase)
- Add: getByText partial-match pitfall — prefer getByRole with exact name
- Add: deck cascade-delete order (schedules → cards → deck in one transaction)
- Add: unscheduled cards are immediately due (key domain invariant)
- Add: React StrictMode double-invoke guard pattern (check-then-write in Dexie transaction)
- Add: stable event listener + refs pattern for keyboard shortcuts (ReviewSession)

implement-issue SKILL.md changes:
- Step 9: add "promote stable lessons to CLAUDE.md" as a routine sub-step with criteria
  for what belongs in CLAUDE.md (technical facts) vs MEMORY.md (workflow meta)
- Description: mention the living-docs responsibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: guard implement-issue skill against stranded commits and unsaved lessons

- Step 3: run `gh pr list --state all --head <branch>` before creating any PR
- Step 7: run `git status` before staging to catch all modified files (skills, etc.)
- Step 8: check `gh pr list` before pushing a follow-up commit — if MERGED, branch to a new PR
- Step 9: save lessons immediately in the same response; stating without saving means they're lost

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: keyboard shortcuts in review + success toast on add card

- Space reveals answer, 1-4 rates (Again/Hard/Good/Easy) in review
- Uses refs so the stable event listener reads live state without re-registering on every render
- AddCardForm shows a 3s "Card added!" status message after successful submission
- 7 new E2E tests covering keyboard shortcuts, success toast, validation edge cases, mobile nav, and card count reactivity
- Fixed false-passing test: getByText('Answer') also matched 'Show Answer' button; now waits for card front text and checks for rating buttons directly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent double default deck on dev reload (StrictMode race)

React StrictMode double-invokes effects; both ensureDefaultDeck() calls
raced past the count===0 guard before either wrote. Fix: use a fixed
deck ID and move the existence check inside a serialized Dexie
transaction so concurrent calls are idempotent.

Also pin vite dev server to port 5173 (strictPort) and kill it before
starting, so orphaned servers from prior sessions don't cause port drift.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace remove_small_objects with cc3d.dust, fix SLURM quoting

- Use cc3d.dust instead of skimage.remove_small_objects for seed
  filtering to eliminate spurious warnings on single-label arrays
- Add exclude parameter to slurm/slurm-sharded recipes
- Fix --wrap quoting by passing command via PYTC_CMD env var
- Sanitize SLURM job name to avoid long paths breaking sbatch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix detail view going blank after saving an update

closeUpdateModal() nulled pendingUpdateId before openDetail() used
it, so the detail view rendered with null. Capture the ID first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bootstrap skipping backend ensure steps on subsequent runs

The cached profile early-return in first_run.run() skipped all backend
ensure steps (Ollama, llama-server, vLLM).  Now the probe and backend
ensure steps always run, while the cached profile is still used for
the tier/model selection.  Also adds a "run concierge bootstrap" hint
to doctor output when backends are unhealthy.

commit.message:
Migrate wakeword training to official openWakeWord pipeline (#42)

The custom train.sh produced models with input shape (batch, 96) which
is incompatible with openwakeword v0.6.0 runtime that feeds (1, 16, 96).

Replace with openwakeword-trainer (lgpearson1771) 13-step pipeline:
- 904-speaker Piper TTS with slerp interpolation (was 4 voices)
- Phoneme-based adversarial negative generation (was 12 hardcoded phrases)
- ACAV100M 2000-hour negative features (was 2400 TTS clips)
- RIR + background noise augmentation (was none)
- 50K-step 3-phase training with hard mining (was 500 steps)
- Correct output shape (1, 16, 96) for runtime compatibility

Add document review flow, channel-first UX, and App Home tab

- Document review: accept any link, run auto-QC on Google Docs, submit to
  triage with report card and Excel spreadsheet. Results stay internal to
  marketing — requesters just get a confirmation.
- Channel-first: bot responds to all messages in dedicated channel, no
  @mention needed. DMs redirect to channel (brand resource questions
  still work in DMs).
- App Home tab: services menu when someone clicks the bot's name.
- Hello → help: greetings show the services menu instead of starting intake.
- Channel topic set on startup for discoverability.
- Simplified recovery: "start over" instead of complex reconstruction.
- QC engine: Claude-powered content review against brand guidelines with
  structured grading (A-F), Excel workbook generation, and triage integration.

fix(chatbot): respeitar flag workingHoursOnly antes de bloquear fora do horário

Ambos webhook.ts e chatbot.ts verificavam o horário de atendimento incondicionalmente,
ignorando o campo workingHoursOnly da config. Agora a mensagem de horário fechado só
é enviada quando workingHoursOnly=true. Com a flag desativada no painel, a IA responde
24/7 normalmente.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix read_file_bytes to return Python bytes (#567)

## Summary
- `read_file_bytes` returns `Vec<u8>` which PyO3 converts to Python
`list[int]`, breaking `.decode()` calls
- Changed return type to `Py<PyBytes>` so it returns proper Python
`bytes`

## Test plan
- [ ] Verify `sandbox.read_file("/path")` returns `bytes` type
- [ ] Verify `.decode("utf-8")` works on the result

commit.message:
feat: add telemetry:numpoints setting for sysinfo chart history length (#98)

* feat: add telemetry:numpoints setting for sysinfo chart history length

Adds a configurable `telemetry:numpoints` setting (30-1024, default 120) that
controls how many data points the sysinfo CPU chart displays. History duration
is numpoints × interval (e.g., 300 points at 1s = 5 minutes).

Changes:
- schema/settings.json: add telemetry:numpoints schema entry
- wconfig.rs: add telemetry_numpoints field to SettingsType
- sysinfo-model.ts: numPoints atom reads from global settings first

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent cmd shell flash when opening settings on Windows

On Windows, open_in_editor was spawning `cmd /C start` which flashed a
visible console window. Use CREATE_NO_WINDOW flag to suppress it, and
skip the CLI editor probing on Windows (use OS default directly).

commit.message:
fix: skip production env validation in CI builds

Next.js sets NODE_ENV=production during `pnpm build`, which triggers
productionRequirements validation for 21 env vars that don't exist in
CI. Check for CI env var (auto-set by GitHub Actions) to skip
production-only checks during CI builds.

commit.message:
feat(hp): Retroactive credit scoring for HirePurchasing payment history (Issue #28)

When a player has been using HirePurchasing with on-time lease payments and
then adds UsedPlus, their existing payment history is now recognized in the
credit score system. On first load, up to 24 retroactive on-time payments
are seeded into PaymentTracker from HP lease data (capped to match vanilla
loan seeding). Persisted via hpRetroactiveSeeded flag to prevent re-seeding.

Also fixes upCreditScore/upListDeals/upListSearches console commands that
crashed on Mac due to g_currentMission.player being nil (now uses getFarmId).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add calibration-driven signal bonus to scoring, fix column highlights

Signal bonus: GREEN entry signals now boost composite score scaled by
calibration edge (0.25 per % point, capped at 5.0). YELLOW gets 60%.
Shows in score tooltip as "Signal: +X.X". Applied on both browser and
server scans.

commit.message:
Charts: Legend props consolidation (#47506)

* Docs: Update .mdx files to reflect legend props consolidation into nested legend object

Update all chart documentation files to reflect the breaking API change
where flat legend* props have been consolidated into a nested legend
config object (ChartLegendConfig) on BaseChartProps.

Mapping:
- legendOrientation → legend.orientation
- legendPosition → legend.position
- legendAlignment → legend.alignment
- legendMaxWidth → legend.labelStyles.maxWidth
- legendTextOverflow → legend.labelStyles.textOverflow
- legendItemClassName → legend.className
- legendShape → legend.shape
- legendInteractive → legend.interactive
- legendShapeStyles → legend.shapeStyles

showLegend, withLegendGlyph, and legendLabels remain as top-level props.

Updated files:
- Bar Chart API reference and docs
- Line Chart API reference and docs
- Pie Chart API reference and docs
- Pie Semi Circle Chart API reference and docs
- Leaderboard Chart API reference and docs
- Legend component docs

Co-authored-by: Adam Wood <adamwoodnz@users.noreply.github.com>

* CHARTS-178: Consolidate flat legend* chart props into nested legend object on BaseChartProps

Replace 8 flat legend* props (legendOrientation, legendPosition, legendAlignment,
legendMaxWidth, legendTextOverflow, legendItemClassName, legendShape,
legendInteractive) with a single structured legend config object.

- Add ChartLegendConfig type to types.ts with all legend configuration options
- Update BaseChartProps to use legend?: ChartLegendConfig instead of flat props
- Keep showLegend as top-level boolean (behavioral toggle, not config)
- Update BarChart, LineChart, PieChart, PieSemiCircleChart, LeaderboardChart
- Remove legendShapeStyles from LeaderboardChartProps (absorbed into legend.shapeStyles)
- Remove legendInteractive from LineChartProps and BarChartProps (now in legend.interactive)
- Update legend-config.tsx story arg types to use dot-notation
- Update all chart stories, tests, and component legend stories
- Add major changelog entry

This is a breaking change for chart-level consumers using the flat legend* props.

Co-authored-by: Adam Wood <adamwoodnz@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix ESLint errors: update JSDoc params and import ordering

* Update changelog: minor significance with breaking change note

* Remove component imports from root types.ts, inline legend style types in ChartLegendConfig

* Move legend style types to root types.ts, import from there in components

* Remove redundant legend type re-exports from component barrel and package index

* Fix Storybook controls for legend props in pie, donut, semi-circle, and leaderboard chart stories

Replace dot-notation argType keys and nested legend args with flat keys
to fix Storybook controls. Use extractLegendConfig() in render functions
to bridge flat args back to the nested legend config object.

- Replace legend: { ... } nested args with flat legendXxx keys
- Replace 'legend.xxx' argType keys with flat legendXxx keys
- Replace args.legend?.xxx accesses with extractLegendConfig(args)
- Make extractLegendConfig generic to support typed chart legend configs

* Fix Storybook legend controls: use flat argType keys with extractLegendConfig helper

* Rename legend.className to legend.itemClassName for clarity

legend.className was ambiguous — Legend component has both className (container)
and itemClassName (individual items). Using itemClassName makes the target explicit.

* Spread all legend config props in composition legend stories

Instead of cherry-picking orientation/alignment/position/labelStyles,
spread the full extractLegendConfig result so shape, interactive,
itemClassName, and shapeStyles all pass through to composition legends.

* Move legendShapeStyles control to shared legend config

* feat(charts): Pass itemStyles and shapeStyles to legend in all chart types

Charts exposed legend.itemStyles and legend.shapeStyles in their props but
weren't forwarding them to ChartLegend. Legend customization was incomplete.

- Add itemStyles and shapeStyles pass-through in bar, line, pie, and
  semi-circle charts
- Add labelStyles, itemClassName, and itemStyles in leaderboard chart
- Add legendItemStyles to shared legend-config for Storybook
- Move legendValueDisplay argType into pie-chart stories (pie-specific)

Made-with: Cursor

* feat(charts): Add legendValueDisplay control to pie-semi-circle stories

Expose legendValueDisplay as a Storybook arg so viewers can test
percentage, value, valueDisplay, and none modes in the InteractiveLegend
story. Also enable the legendInteractive control in WithCompositionLegend
by removing its argTypes override.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Wire up legend controls in story metas

Pie, donut, semi-circle, and leaderboard chart stories were
missing meta-level render functions that map flat Storybook
args (legendPosition, etc.) to the nested `legend` prop via
extractLegendConfig. Without this, legend controls in the
Storybook panel were silently ignored for those charts. Bar
and line charts already had this wiring.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Enable interactive legends in composition stories

Composition legend stories were missing chartId props, which are
required for interactive legends to track series visibility via
GlobalChartsProvider. They also had legendInteractive controls
explicitly disabled in Storybook, preventing users from testing
interactivity in those stories.

Also removes an incorrect note from the pie chart API docs
claiming interactive legends only work with props-based legends.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Adam Wood <adamwoodnz@users.noreply.github.com>

ENH: Extract PDF text to reduce LLM hallucination (#10)

* ENH: Extract PDF text to reduce LLM hallucination in notebook generation

Feed full paper text (extracted via pymupdf4llm) to both the research
and notebook generation LLMs so they ground responses in actual paper
content rather than hallucinating from training data.

Key changes:
- Add pymupdf4llm dependency for markdown-optimized PDF text extraction
- Extract download_pdf() as standalone reusable function
- Add extract_text_from_pdf() with section stripping (references,
  bibliography, acknowledgments) and section-aware truncation
- Thread paper_text parameter through generator pipeline
- Update system prompts with grounding instructions
- Wire pipeline in fastapi_app with asyncio.to_thread for non-blocking
  PDF text extraction and graceful degradation on failure

- Extract style prop from Input spread to merge it instead of replacing themed styles
- Add flex: 1 + marginRight to RunListItem headerLeft so XP badge stays visible

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use bucket:file format and is_quick_export for IMS export

The IMS export API in sa-argentina-1 (and LATAM regions) stores all
images in UDS internal storage with lazy loading. The standard export
call with obs://bucket/file fails with IMG.0072 because the image
data is not materialized.

The fix uses is_quick_export=true and bucket_url in the format
"bucket:filename" (without obs:// prefix), which is the format used
by the HWC console and works with UDS/lazy-loaded images.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Standardize data directory to ~/.local/reg-rs/ with REG_RS_DATA_DIR override

Centralize test database storage in ~/.local/reg-rs/ instead of requiring
a project-local data/ directory. The data directory is now configurable
via the REG_RS_DATA_DIR environment variable, which tests and demos use
to isolate their data in ./work/reg-rs/ (git-ignored) so self-tests and
integration tests never pollute the user's production data.

Key changes:
- Add data_dir() in lib.rs: checks REG_RS_DATA_DIR, then ~/.local/reg-rs/,
  then ./data/ as fallback
- Add resolve_test_path() in command.rs: auto-places tests in data dir
  and appends .tdb extension when given a bare name
- Update finder.rs and status/server.rs to use centralized data_dir()
- Integration tests pass REG_RS_DATA_DIR=./work/reg-rs to subprocesses
- Update all help text, docs, demo scripts, and VHS tapes to use simple
  test names instead of data/ paths

commit.message:
Phase 5: QA integration tests — 29 tests verifying full pipeline

Integration tests covering:
- Full mock pipeline: scenario -> runner -> evaluator -> reporter -> JSON report
- Component interface wiring: audio format compatibility, type contracts
  between RecoClient, ScenarioRunner, Evaluator, Reporter
- Scenario YAML parsing: edge cases, defaults, all field types, directory loading
- Error handling: call timeout produces partial result, not crash
- Mock mode works with zero env vars
- No circular imports across all modules
- CLI smoke tests (help, single file, directory mode)

Verification results:
- 242/242 tests pass (213 existing + 29 new integration)
- CLI mock pipeline runs end-to-end
- JSON reports are valid and parseable
- All component interfaces are compatible

No bugs found — all interfaces match, mock mode is fully functional.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix JSON injection and path traversal vulnerabilities in hooks

Security fixes identified by Gemini Code Assist review of PR #2:

1. JSON injection (all 4 hooks): block() used string interpolation
   to build JSON. Quotes or backslashes in paths broke output.
   Fix: use jq -n --arg for safe JSON construction.

2. Path traversal (file-guard): only stripped ./ prefix, did not
   resolve .. components. subdir/../.env bypassed .env protection.
   Fix: normalize_path() collapses .. segments before matching.
   Compatible with bash 3.2 (macOS) and set -u (nounset).

9 new test assertions for traversal and JSON validity. 390 tests total.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
